course_description:
This course provides a current overview of cutting-edge innovation in biomanufacturing biotechnology. We will cover, in detail, specific enabling technologies driving change in biomanufacturing industries. This includes, but not limited to:

Students will learn about the current global trends in biomanufacturing and how cutting-edge biomanufacturing tools are changing industries to meet demand.

This is a new course, offered for the first time in 2025. The course is designed to build on the basic information provided in courses such as BIOT7018 (Biologics) to explore how critical new pharmaceutically and industrially important biological molecules are manufactured on a large scale. We will explore the importance of enzymes in biomanufacturing, including methods for optimisation of their use in industry. We will learn about large scale production of proteins and then explore in detail the processing and analysis of these biomolecules that are critical in industrial environments. The final module will be delivered by industry experts in GMP, regulatory issues in biomanufacturing and quality control necessary in the industry.

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
The 2 hours of lectures per week are timetabled as a 2 hour block. There is also a 2 hour workshop each week. Both sessions are interactive and involve active learning activities. Attendance at both sessions is expected. The lectures will be recorded, the workshops WILL NOT be recorded.
course_aims:
This course aims to allow students to:

a) gain an understanding of the theoretical and practical aspects of large scale protein expression technologies, cell line engineering and other disruptive biomanufacturing technologies.

b) become familiar with the upstream and downstream bioprocessing necessary in biomanufacturing and the optimisation and interconnectedness of these processes.

c) become proficient in the performance and evaluation of analytical techniques critical in the quality control and assurance of biomanufacturing of biologics.

d) become familiar with the regulatory issues and processes necessary to the commercialisation of biologics

e) become familiar with the requirements of cGMP in the biomanufacturing industry and the importance of adherence to these requirements in the workplace.

learning_outcomes.description: Use their knowledge and expertise to evaluate biomanufacturing technologies and trouble shoot expression technologies.
Critically analyse and evaluate new biomanufacturing processes.

Identify and explain the downstream processing required for large scale manufacture of a biological molecule with an understanding of the purpose and importance of each stage of the manufacturing process.
Perform and analyse data generated using key analytical techniques critical in the evaluation of biomanufacturing products and procedures.
Identify the requirements of cGMP in biomanufacturing and critically evaluate scenarios and case studies for compliance to these standards.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 3 (28 Jul - 17 Aug)
learning_activities.topic: MODULE 1: Enzyme engineering and cell free processes in Biomanufacturing. Each week will consist of 2 hours of lecture and a 2 hour workshop. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 4 To Week 6 (18 Aug - 07 Sep)
learning_activities.topic: MODULE 2: Biomanufacturing of therapeutic biologics. Each week will consist of 2 hours of lecture and a 2 hour workshop. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 7 To Week 9 (08 Sep - 28 Sep)
learning_activities.topic: MODULE 3: Downstream processing and purification at scale. Each week will consist of 2 hours of lecture and a 2 hour workshop. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 10 To Week 12 (06 Oct - 26 Oct)
learning_activities.topic: MODULE 4: GMP, regulatory affairs and analytical techniques in biomanufacturing. Each week will consist of 2 hours of lecture and a 2 hour workshop. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05

Site visits and case studies Class visits to one or more industrial biomanufacturing sites will be arranged. Real life case studies and scenarios will be explored on these visits. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
